reasoning: Liu Zige's 200m long course butterfly world record has stood since 2009. While Summer McIntosh recently came within 0.18 seconds of the record at the July 2025 World Championships, athletes peak and taper for summer championships. The window between November and February is almost entirely dedicated to short-course (25m) competitions (like the December Short Course World Championships) or heavy early-season training. Breaking a daunting 'super-suit' world record requires a full taper and optimal form, which is exceedingly rare in mid-season long-course meets. Thus, it is highly likely the record will survive through February 2026.
